Premium Audit and Accounting Conditions

LG695378
04/01/2026 - 04/01/2027
Premium Audit and Accounting Conditions
BILLING policy form Policy No. LG695378
ItemCGL-0002
Class / Territory41670 / 007
ExposureGross sales 107,692
LimitGeneral Aggregate: $2,000,000
Premium$5,546
FormCG 20 10 04 13

Coverage Part Conditions

This page applies to classification 41670 at Location 2 as shown in the declarations, classification schedule, and exposure rating schedule.

Classification, territory, and exposure entries are rating information and do not create coverage by themselves.

Limits, exclusions, additional insured status, and completed-operations terms are controlled by the applicable forms and endorsements.

Records supporting sales, payroll, area, contracts, leases, or operations must be retained for audit and coverage review.

Any endorsement listed for a scheduled item applies according to its own terms and effective date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 41670 at Location 2 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

Section A - Schedule References

The declarations identify class code 41670 at Location 2 with gross sales of 107,692; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Coverage Part Conditions

This page applies to classification 91560 at Location 6 as shown in the declarations, classification schedule, and exposure rating schedule.

Classification, territory, and exposure entries are rating information and do not create coverage by themselves.

Limits, exclusions, additional insured status, and completed-operations terms are controlled by the applicable forms and endorsements.

Records supporting sales, payroll, area, contracts, leases, or operations must be retained for audit and coverage review.

Any endorsement listed for a scheduled item applies according to its own terms and effective date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 91560 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

Section A - Schedule References

The declarations identify class code 91560 at Location 6 with payroll of 560,644; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Any endorsement listed for a scheduled item applies according to its own terms and effective date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 16916 at Location 5 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

Section A - Schedule References

The declarations identify class code 16916 at Location 5 with cost of work of 858,689; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC
AMENDATORY

State Amendatory Endorsement

LG695378
04/01/2026 - 04/01/2027
State Amendatory Endorsement
AMENDATORY policy form Policy No. LG695378
ItemCGL-0060
Class / Territory91560 / 001
ExposurePayroll 878,612
LimitMedical Expense: $5,000
Premium$45,248
FormCG 21 06 01 24

Coverage Part Conditions

This page applies to classification 91560 at Location 6 as shown in the declarations, classification schedule, and exposure rating schedule.

Classification, territory, and exposure entries are rating information and do not create coverage by themselves.

Limits, exclusions, additional insured status, and completed-operations terms are controlled by the applicable forms and endorsements.

Records supporting sales, payroll, area, contracts, leases, or operations must be retained for audit and coverage review.

Any endorsement listed for a scheduled item applies according to its own terms and effective date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 91560 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

Section A - Schedule References

The declarations identify class code 91560 at Location 6 with payroll of 878,612; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC
AMENDATORY
Juniper Crown Casualty | Commercial General Liability

This page applies to classification 61217 at Location 2 as shown in the declarations, classification schedule, and exposure rating schedule.

Classification, territory, and exposure entries are rating information and do not create coverage by themselves.

Limits, exclusions, additional insured status, and completed-operations terms are controlled by the applicable forms and endorsements.

Records supporting sales, payroll, area, contracts, leases, or operations must be retained for audit and coverage review.

Any endorsement listed for a scheduled item applies according to its own terms and effective date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 61217 at Location 2 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Material Policy Provisions

LG695378
04/01/2026 - 04/01/2027
Material provisions in this section apply to the current policy term. Similar prior-term or advisory notes in archive pages are retained for underwriting reference.

Classification And Territory Limitation

For class 10010 (Mercantile - food or beverage stores) at Location 1, with PFAS Exclusion, the applicable form is CG 00 01 edition 01 24 and the provision is treated as limitation.

The Mercantile - food or beverage stores operation applies at Location 1, class 10010, territory 003 only when the declarations show the same exposure basis.

Aggregate Limit Application

The provision below is read with CG 00 01 edition 01 24 for Location 1, class 10010, territory 003.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 applies with the Commercial General Liability coverage part and does not increase another aggregate.

Additional Insured Contract Gate

CG 00 01 edition 01 24 is a condition provision for Location 1, class 10010, territory 003.

Additional insured status for Location 1, class 10010, territory 003 applies only when the contract requirement and attached form CG 00 01 are both satisfied.

Exclusion And Endorsement Priority

For class 10010 (Mercantile - food or beverage stores) at Location 1, with PFAS Exclusion, the applicable form is CG 00 01 edition 01 24 and the provision is treated as exclusion.

The PFAS Exclusion provision and form CG 00 01 edition 01 24 control before any conflicting summary entry for Location 1, class 10010, territory 003.

Classification And Territory Limitation

The provision below is read with CG 20 10 edition 04 13 for Location 2, class 41670, territory 007.

The Restaurants - with sales of alcoholic beverages operation applies at Location 2, class 41670, territory 007 only when the declarations show the same exposure basis.

Aggregate Limit Application

CG 20 10 edition 04 13 is a condition provision for Location 2, class 41670, territory 007.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 applies with the Commercial General Liability coverage part and does not increase another aggregate.

Additional Insured Contract Gate

For class 41670 (Restaurants - with sales of alcoholic beverages) at Location 2, with Silica or Silica-Related Dust Exclusion, the applicable form is CG 20 10 edition 04 13 and the provision is treated as condition.

Additional insured status for Location 2, class 41670, territory 007 applies only when the contract requirement and attached form CG 20 10 are both satisfied.

Exclusion And Endorsement Priority

The provision below is read with CG 20 10 edition 04 13 for Location 2, class 41670, territory 007.

The Silica or Silica-Related Dust Exclusion provision and form CG 20 10 edition 04 13 control before any conflicting summary entry for Location 2, class 41670, territory 007.

Classification And Territory Limitation

CG 20 37 edition 12 19 is a limitation provision for Location 3, class 16916, territory 007.

The Contractors - subcontracted work operation applies at Location 3, class 16916, territory 007 only when the declarations show the same exposure basis.

Aggregate Limit Application

For class 16916 (Contractors - subcontracted work) at Location 3, with Data Privacy Violation Exclusion, the applicable form is CG 20 37 edition 12 19 and the provision is treated as condition.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 applies with the Commercial General Liability coverage part and does not increase another aggregate.

Additional Insured Contract Gate

The provision below is read with CG 20 37 edition 12 19 for Location 3, class 16916, territory 007.

Additional insured status for Location 3, class 16916, territory 007 applies only when the contract requirement and attached form CG 20 37 are both satisfied.

Exclusion And Endorsement Priority

CG 20 37 edition 12 19 is a exclusion provision for Location 3, class 16916, territory 007.

The Data Privacy Violation Exclusion provision and form CG 20 37 edition 12 19 control before any conflicting summary entry for Location 3, class 16916, territory 007.

Classification And Territory Limitation

For class 91560 (Concrete construction) at Location 4, with Employment-Related Practices Exclusion, the applicable form is CG 21 06 edition 09 21 and the provision is treated as limitation.

The Concrete construction operation applies at Location 4, class 91560, territory 003 only when the declarations show the same exposure basis.

Aggregate Limit Application

The provision below is read with CG 21 06 edition 09 21 for Location 4, class 91560, territory 003.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies with the Commercial General Liability coverage part and does not increase another aggregate.

LG695378
04/01/2026 - 04/01/2027
Policy Conditions
POLICY_FORM policy form Policy No. LG695378
ItemCGL-0036
Class / Territory91560 / 003
ExposurePayroll 560,644
LimitMedical Expense: $5,000
Premium$6,279
FormCG 00 01 09 21

Coverage Part Conditions

This page applies to classification 91560 at Location 6 as shown in the declarations, classification schedule, and exposure rating schedule.

Classification, territory, and exposure entries are rating information and do not create coverage by themselves.

Limits, exclusions, additional insured status, and completed-operations terms are controlled by the applicable forms and endorsements.

Records supporting sales, payroll, area, contracts, leases, or operations must be retained for audit and coverage review.

Any endorsement listed for a scheduled item applies according to its own terms and effective date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 91560 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

Section A - Schedule References

The declarations identify class code 91560 at Location 6 with payroll of 560,644; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC

Juniper Crown Casualty | Commercial General Liability

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Limits, exclusions, additional insured status, and completed-operations terms are controlled by the applicable forms and endorsements.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

This page applies to classification 18435 at Location 3 as shown in the declarations, classification schedule, and exposure rating schedule.

Classification, territory, and exposure entries are rating information and do not create coverage by themselves.

Limits, exclusions, additional insured status, and completed-operations terms are controlled by the applicable forms and endorsements.

Records supporting sales, payroll, area, contracts, leases, or operations must be retained for audit and coverage review.

Any endorsement listed for a scheduled item applies according to its own terms and effective date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 18435 at Location 3 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

Section B - Limits And Aggregates

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

This page applies to classification 97047 at Location 5 as shown in the declarations, classification schedule, and exposure rating schedule.

Classification, territory, and exposure entries are rating information and do not create coverage by themselves.

Limits, exclusions, additional insured status, and completed-operations terms are controlled by the applicable forms and endorsements.

Records supporting sales, payroll, area, contracts, leases, or operations must be retained for audit and coverage review.

Any endorsement listed for a scheduled item applies according to its own terms and effective date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 97047 at Location 5 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.
